data.title: Chinese tech firms said to train AI models abroad to tap Nvidia chips
data.description:
Chinese tech companies are training their artificial intelligence models overseas to access Nvidia's (NVDA) chips, the Financial Times reported, citing two people with direct knowledge of the matter. 

Companies such as Alibaba (BABA) and ByteDance (BDNCE) are said to be training their latest AI models in Southeast Asian data centers. The companies rely on lease agreements for data centers owned and operated by non-Chinese entities.

The shift in AI training is aimed at bypassing U.S. measures to restrict sales of Nvidia's (NVDA) chips, like the H20, in China.

Notably, DeepSeek's (DEEPSEEK) model is being trained in China as the AI startup reportedly secured a large stock of Nvidia (NVDA) chips before the U.S. imposed export controls.

DeepSeek (DEEPSEEK) is also working with local chipmakers like Huawei to develop the next generation of Chinese AI chips.

The report comes as China has been discouraging local tech companies from buying advanced Nvidia (NVDA) chips, and recently banned the use of foreign AI chips in state-funded data centers.

Meanwhile, President Donald Trump is considering whether to let Nvidia sell H200 chips to China.

data.title: World Copper appoints Mark Lotz as new CEO and President
Leafly names Peter Lee as CEO, replacing Yoko Miyashita
ALT5 Sigma announces major leadership shakeup: Acting CEO, CFO, COO depart as new executives appointed
Asia stocks higher tracking Wall Street's tech recovery; China shares buoyed by stimulus hopes despite Vanke losses
data.description:
Asia stocks trade higher on Thursday after Wall Street overnight logged its fourth straight advance on growing expectations of a Fed rate cut in December.  Chinese shares also contributed to the regional strength, buoyed by expectations that Beijing will introduce further stimulus measures to support the economy. Investors are banking on greater government intervention to stabilize China growth. However, the gains in China were partially constrained by persistent instability in the real estate sector.

Gold prices dipped to around $4,150 per ounce on Thursday but remained near a two-week-high. Gold is on track for a fourth consecutive monthly gain, having risen nearly 60% this year and poised for its best annual performance since 1979.

Malaysia’s producer prices fell 0.1% year-on-year in October 2025, easing from a 0.8% decline in the previous month and marking the mildest drop in an eight-month streak of decreases.

The Bank of Korea maintained its base rate at 2.50% in November for the fourth consecutive meeting, the final policy session of the year, as expected.

Retail sales in New Zealand rose 1.9% quarter-on-quarter in the three months to September 2025, rising from a 0.5% increase in the June quarter.

Japan (NKY:IND) rose 1.16% to above 50,100 while the broader Topix Index added 0.7% to 3,380 on Thursday, extending gains from the previous session. The Japanese yen strengthened past 156 per dollar on Thursday, reversing the prior session’s losses.

China (SHCOMP) rose 0.55% to above 3880, while the Shenzhen Component gained 1.2% to 13060 on Thursday, with mainland shares hitting one-week highs, as gains in technology and industrial stocks buoyed mainland markets, but property shares fell amid renewed debt crisis concerns, limiting overall advancement, and the offshore yuan weakened to around 7.07 per dollar, retreating from a thirteen-month high reached in the previous session following the People’s Bank of China’s first weaker-than-expected fixing in months.

Profits at China’s industrial firms rose 1.9% y/y to CNY 5.95 trillion in the first ten months of 2025, easing from a 3.2% growth in the prior period as demand softened and pricing pressures persisted. 

Investors also monitored reports that state-backed developer China Vanke is seeking to delay for the first time an onshore bond repayment due December 15, raising renewed concerns over policy support for the troubled property sector. 

Meanwhile, investors are awaiting the upcoming PMI releases due this weekend for fresh insights into China’s economic momentum.

Investors also looked forward to China’s Central Economic Work Conference in December for fresh policy signals for the year ahead.

Hong Kong (HSI) rose 0.37% to 26,012 in early deals on Thursday, increasing for a fourth session.

India (SENSEX) rose 0.37% to 85,909, while the Nifty 50 climbed to 26,280, with both indices hitting record highs in Thursday morning trade. 

Australia (AS51) rose 0.08% to around 8,640 in Thursday morning trade, marking its fourth consecutive session of gain to hit a two-week-high. 

Total new capital expenditure in Australia grew by 6.4% q/q in Q3 2025, surpassing market expectations of a 0.5% expansion.

Private new capital expenditure on buildings and structures in Australia rose by 2.1% QoQ in Q3 2025, accelerating from a 0.2% gain in Q2 to the fastest pace in a year. 

Private new capital expenditure on equipment, plant, and machinery in Australia expanded by 11.5% quarter-on-quarter in the three months to September 2025

In the U.S. on Wednesday, all three major indexes ended higher, extending gains for a fourth straight session on Wednesday as the market pushed into the Thanksgiving week, driven by a sharp repricing of Fed policy that now puts the odds of a 25bp December cut above 80%. 

Trading volume is expected to stay light through Friday due to the Thanksgiving holiday.

U.S. stock futures on Thursday: Dow +0.13%; S&P 500 +0.07%; Nasdaq +0.13%.

Currencies: (JPY:USD), (CNY:USD), (AUD:USD), (INR:USD), (HKD:USD), (NZD:USD).

data.title: Omnicom raises dividend by 14.3% to $0.80 a share
data.description:
• Omnicom (OMC) declares $0.80/share quarterly dividend, 14.3% increase from prior dividend of $0.70.
  • Forward yield 4.48%
  • Payable Jan. 9; to shareholders of record Dec. 19; ex-div Dec. 19.
  • The company raised its quarterly dividend by 14.3% after paying a quarterly dividend of $0.70 in each of the previous 20 quarters.
  • See OMC Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Crude oil rebounds as tug-of-war over Ukraine peace deal vs. Fed rate cut continues
data.description:
Crude oil futures on Wednesday bounced off one-month lows reached in the previous session, extending the recent tug of war between the possibility of a Russia-Ukraine peace deal that would open the door to lifting sanctions on Russian energy exports and optimism over a potential Federal Reserve rate cut in December that could boost global fuel demand.

Prices were unfazed by bearish-looking U.S. crude and product stock builds, as the Energy Information Administration reported domestic crude inventories increased by a much larger than expected 2.8 million barrels to 426.9 million barrels last week, ~4% below the five-year average for the time of year, as imports surged.

Gasoline and distillate inventories also rose more than expected, with respeective increases of 2.5 million barrels to 209.9 million barrels, 3% below the five-year average, and 1.1 million barrels to 112.2 million barrels, 5% below the five-year average, according to the EIA.

"The crude oil market is navigating a period marked by multiple downward pressures: elevated U.S. inventories, signs of oversupply, steady OPEC+ production, and diplomatic progress between Russia and Ukraine," Antonio Di Giacomo of XS.com said in a note. "Although Brent and WTI prices have stabilized temporarily, the short- and medium-term outlook remains tilted to the downside."

The two major crude oil benchmarks rose for the second time in three days, with front-month Nymex crude (CL1:COM) for January delivery settling +1.21% to $58.65/bbl, and front-month Brent crude (CO1:COM) for January finishing +1% to $63.13/bbl.

U.S. natural gas (NG1:COM) gained ahead of the Thanksgiving holiday, supported by colder weather expected this weekend and next week, while the EIA's weekly storage report was mixed, as the 11 Bcf withdrawal to 3,935 Bcf was more than expected but below the five-year average draw of 25 Bcf; the new January front-month Nymex contract closed +1.7% to $4.558/MMBtu.

data.title: Anglo-Teck merger wins support from ISS, Glass Lewis
data.description:
Teck Resources (TECK) said Wednesday that independent proxy advisory firms Institutional Shareholder Services and Glass Lewis both recommended its shareholders vote in favor of the company’s merger with Anglo American (OTCQX:AAUKF) (OTCQX:NGLOY).

"The arrangement makes strategic sense in light of the anticipated synergies, strategic benefits, and opportunity for additional upside through ownership in the combined company," ISS said in its report. "The universe of potential buyers is limited, the board actively explored alternative transaction structures in order to maximize shareholder value, shareholders are expected to benefit from increased liquidity and stronger financial position for the combined company, and the market reaction has been positive."

The ISS recommendation comes a few days after Glass Lewis recommended voting for the deal, saying the merger terms "appear reasonable for Teck shareholders," and provide an "opportunity to participate in a substantially larger and more diversified critical minerals group with enhanced copper exposure, significant disclosed synergy potential and a broader long-term growth profile than Teck could achieve on a standalone basis."

Canada Industry Minister Melanie Joly said Wednesday the proposed merger will be submitted to a ​national security review, with a final decision expected in the coming months.

The merger still needs approval from regulators in countries including Canada, China and the U.S.; Canada reportedly has been pressuring Anglo (OTCQX:AAUKF) (OTCQX:NGLOY) for stronger commitments to executive and management jobs at its proposed Vancouver headquarters (I, II).

data.title: Ellington financial announces estimated book value per common share of $13.25
data.description:
• Ellington Financial  (EFC) announced on Wednesday an estimated book value per share of common stock of $13.25 as of October 31, 2025. This estimate includes the effect of the previously announced monthly dividend of $0.13 per share of common stock, to be paid on November 28, 2025, to holders of record on October 31, 2025, with the same ex-dividend date.
  • EFC +0.58% after hours to $14.022.
  • Source: Press Release
